rulings.Case Title: ALAN BARTHOLOMEW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 10, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Judgement is granted. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. His motions to strike and evidentiary objections to declaration of Ruelas and Tompkins are denied and overruled. Defendant's request for Judicial Notice (reply Brief, Page 5) is denied as irrelevant. Defendant's motion to strike page 2, lines 8-16, 24 - page 3, line 4, and Page 3, lines 12-18 of plaintiff's March 7, 2009 declaration is granted. The Court observes that none of plaintiff's experts rebut the presumption of law that plaintiff's employer was an experienced, expert stevedore upon whose judgment defendant was entitled to rely regarding vessel asbestos hazards. =(302KOPP/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN BARTHOLOMEW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 10, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT NATIONAL STEEL & SHIPBUILDING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion Granted. Plaintiff shows no knowledge of maintenance history of any Santa Ana equipment he worked with or exposure to asbestos from an origally installed product. Defendant's objection to Ay's declaration is sustained. It lacks foundation, is replete with hearsay and is speculative. =(302KOPP/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN BARTHOLOMEW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: GRANT COOK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 10, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S (1) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. (2) Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to the first and fourth causes of action for failure of defendant to shift burden. There exists a triable issue of material fact as to exposure/causation. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to second and third causes of action. Plaintiffs to present evidence that Defendant produced the insulation. Motion for Summary Ajudicatin is granted regarding punitive damage claim. (Plaintiff's depostition and declaration are not contradictory in any material respect) this declaration does not constitute hearsay. Defendant's five evidentiary objections are overruled. So, too, are defendants three evidentiary objections to the Ay declaration. =(302KOPP/PB)
